summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a classic 1976 Roosevelt show with smoking Help>Slip>Franklin's, soaring Sugaree and a powerful Other One.
review: The recording is a soundboard (SBD) that, while occasionally erratic and bass‑heavy, delivers clear instrument separation and even highlights Phil Lesh’s unusually forward bass. Musically the show shines – the second‑set Help>Slip>Franklin's combo, a 14‑minute Sugaree, a fiery Other One and tight Wheel‑Samson are repeatedly praised, making this a standout summer ’76 performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars and cite multiple highlight songs; recording issues are noted but not enough to outweigh the musical excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear SBD with occasional bass‑heavy, erratic patches (AUD artifacts) and some “bassy” moments; many reviewers praise the mix for bringing out Phil’s bass and audience ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, relaxed first set followed by a second‑set that builds into the classic Help‑Slip‑Frank combo and a high‑energy Wheel‑Samson transition; overall pacing described as “smoking” and “groovy”.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry's solos are repeatedly called flowing, effortless and “coating the walls”; reviewers note he loved playing this venue.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is unusually loud and front‑center – praised as “pounding”, “leading the way” and a highlight of the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum fills described as absurdly energetic; rhythm section noted as still getting used to functioning together but in a good way.
